sources.name: HarmonyOS AI Skill knowledge pack
sources.notes: Curated subset of the 4450-line knowledge pack rewritten into per-skill references (platform baseline, ArkUI components, state management, Kits, permissions, performance, testing). API 21/SDK 6.0.1-era content kept only as version-labeled error recipes; each adapted file carries a source line and a docs-verification header.

sources.name: HarmonyOS practice skills (chen_jeff)
sources.license: MulanPSL-2.0 treated as authoritative from LICENSE file; README states MIT inconsistently
sources.notes: Production pitfall skills (state-tracking chains, service cards, atomic services, signing/submission, IAP) rewritten into references with their verification methods preserved. No upstream files are vendored verbatim. Regulatory and store-policy claims are scoped and require current official verification. Symptom-style trigger phrasing adopted for skill descriptions.

sources.name: DevEco CLI
sources.notes: Official unified CLI referenced as a tool surface (build/run/log/emulator state/docs search/MCP syntax check) in the debugger, create-project, error-fix, and multidevice skills. No code vendored; command quick-reference adapted from its SKILL.md.

sources.name: OpenAI Build iOS Apps
sources.notes: Task-oriented layout and evidence-gated workflows; no Apple-specific code or assets vendored.

sources.name: DevEco CodeGenie skills
sources.license: MIT with Apache-2.0 notices on individual runtime scripts
sources.notes: Concepts and source-separation rules adapted. CodeGenie-only tools were replaced by Codex MCP and portable scripts. API 17-22 scaffolding was not copied into this API 26-aware plugin.

sources.name: OpenHarmony Skills practice guide
sources.notes: Used for quality dimensions, risk calibration, and eval design. Not vendored because repository license status was unclear at review time.
